svg.text: HINDI + RAJASTHANI — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 25D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. KABIR — DOHA (15TH C. CE) —
काल करे सो आज कर, आज करे सो अब ।
kāl kare so āj kar, āj kare so ab.
Tomorrow-do that today-do · today-do that now.
"Do tomorrow’s work today; do today’s work now." — Kabir Das. Hindi’s most beloved sant-poet.
— II. HINDI PROVERB — ON KNOWLEDGE —
विद्या धन सब धनों की दादी है।
vidyā dhan sab dhanoṅ kī dādī hai.
"Knowledge is the greatest of all wealth." — Hindi proverb.
— III. HINDI BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
यह आपके लिए बनाया गया था, मुफ़्त में,
उन लोगों ने जिन्होंने भविष्य को याद किया।
Yah āpke lie banāyā gayā thā, muft meṅ, / un logoṅ ne jinhonnē bhavishy ko yād kiyā.
"This was made for you, freely, by those who remembered the future." (Hindi)
— HINDI NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Bhavishy (भविष्य) = future (Sanskrit: what is to become). Yād karnā = to remember (Urdu/Hindi: memory-do).
Muft = free of charge (from Persian/Arabic muft). Ergative -ne: jinhonnē (those who) marks agent of past act.
SECONDARY — RAJASTHANI — RUNNING TEXT
— I. MEERA BAI — BHAJAN (16TH C. CE, RAJASTHANI/BRAJ) —
पायो जी मैंने राम रतन धन पायो।
pāyo jī main-ne Rām ratan dhan pāyo.
"I have found the jewel-wealth of Ram’s name." — Meera Bai, Rajput princess and poet-saint.
— II. RAJASTHANI PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND LAND —
Dharati mā sō chhē, dharaṇo vālō nā bhūlo.
Earth mother is earth-giving (those) not-forget.
"The earth is mother; those who give earth must not be forgotten." (Rajasthani proverb on land and memory)
— III. RAJASTHANI BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Yē āpāṅrē vāstē banāyō gāyō, muft mēṅ,
unā māṅkhāṅ dvārā jōhāṅ bhavishy nē yād rākhyō.
This for-you made was freely / those people by who-had future remembered-kept.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Rajasthani)
— RAJASTHANI NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Āvaṇero = the future (lit. “the one that is coming/arriving” — present participle of āvanu = to come).
Yād rākhvuṅ = to keep in memory (remember). Literal: memory-keep. Same root as Hindi yād.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — TWO NORTH INDIAN SISTERS —
Hindi: ~600M speakers (L1+L2). Official language of India. Written in Devanagari. National prestige.
Rajasthani: ~70-80M speakers. No official status. Preserved in oral poetry, folk music, and domestic life.
Meera Bai, Kabir, and the bhakti poet-saints wrote in Rajasthani/Braj — not standard Hindi. Literature predates Hindi.

THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
